title: test naming: standardize verb pattern in message_set_update tests
description: Tests 30-35 have inconsistent naming: test 30 uses 'returns_typed_response', test 31 uses 'sends_correct_patch', test 32 reverts to 'returns_typed_response'. The distinction (returns response vs sends patch in request) makes sense semantically, but it breaks scanning consistency. Recommend: (1) standardize to 'returns_typed_response' for all, OR (2) use 'sends_correct_*' for all and rename test 30 to 'message_set_update_body_edit_sends_correct_patch'. Option 2 is more precise since we're testing the request body, not the response.
close_reason: Design decision: test naming is adequate; cosmetic only
